Maternal-Fetal Immunology

  • About

    Why doesn't the maternal immune system reject the fetus?

    A mother's body shelters a genetically distinct human for nine months - tolerated and protected by the very immune system that would reject a transplanted organ. Seventy years after Medawar first asked why, the answer still eludes us.

    Explore the science →
    The course · 6 parts

    A path through the science

    Whether you're new to immunology or just this topic, each section is a quick 15-minute read, with videos and references to explore.

    Part 01 ↗

    Immunology Foundations

    Self vs. non-self, innate vs. adaptive immunity, and how the body learns not to attack itself.
    Part 02 ↗

    The Puzzle of Pregnancy

    Why the fetus isn't rejected, and how the uterus and placenta actively negotiate tolerance.
    Part 03 ↗

    Model Systems

    How cancer and autoimmunity illuminate the fundamental mechanisms of tolerance.
    Part 04 ↗

    When Tolerance Breaks Down

    Recurrent miscarriage, preeclampsia, and preterm birth: the immunological roots of obstetric disease.
    Part 05 ↗

    Applications Beyond Pregnancy

    What pregnancy teaches us about cancer, transplants, and autoimmunity.
    Part 06 ↗

    Frontiers & Why We Need You

    Open questions, cutting-edge tools, and how to get involved.
    From the field

    Conversations with researchers at the maternal–fetal interface.

    Researchers and clinicians working on the frontiers of maternal-fetal immunology share what drew them to this field, what they're investigating, and where they think the science is headed.

    Read all interviews →
    For early college students

    This field needs you.

    Maternal-fetal immunology is one of the most underexplored areas in biology. The clinicians treating preeclampsia, recurrent miscarriage, and preterm birth every day are waiting for scientists to come work alongside them. We made this site so you'd know it exists.

    See research opportunities Read interviews
    ~3.6M
    births / yr in the US
    1 in 25
    pregnancies hit by preeclampsia
    Only 8.8%
    of NIH funding for women's health (2013–2023)
    1953
    when Medawar asked why pregnancy doesn't reject the fetus. Answer: still unknown
